refactoring-oop-services.label: Refactoring OOP Services
refactoring-oop-services.summary: Move coordination logic into services while keeping model classes responsible for their own behavior.

sketch1.title: Refactoring OOP Services: the file shape

sketch2.title: Refactoring OOP Services: what the grader checks

ci-refactor-library-practice.title: Refactor library item reporting
ci-refactor-library-practice.prompt: Move mixed item reporting into a service that calls each object’s `label()`.
ci-refactor-library-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-refactor-library-practice.starterCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.video import Video
from services.catalog import catalog_lines

items = [Book("Python"), Video("OOP")]
for line in catalog_lines(items):
    print(line)

sc-refactoring-oop-services-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-refactoring-oop-services-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for refactoring oop services?
sc-refactoring-oop-services-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Move coordination logic into services while keeping model classes responsible for their own behavior.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch0.title: Move reporting into a service file
try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch0.starterCode:
from models.task import Task
from services.reports import task_report

tasks = [Task("Plan", False), Task("Ship", True)]
print(task_report(tasks))

try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch1.title: Keep main.py thin after refactor
try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-refactoring-oop-services-sketch1.starterCode:
from models.order import Order
from services.orders import order_summary

orders = [Order("A1", 20), Order("B2", 35)]
print(order_summary(orders))

concept.title: Refactoring OOP Services: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Refactoring OOP Services** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Move coordination logic into services while keeping model classes responsible for their own behavior.

multifile-shape.title: Refactoring OOP Services: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Refactoring OOP Services: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
